summary: Document Management Service — Data Layer Foundation
description:
Establish the complete data persistence layer for the document-service. This epic covers all configuration, schema, and ORM work required before document upload, verification, and S3 integration features can be built. It spans service profile configuration, Flyway-managed schema creation for application_documents, document_requirements, and document_verification_audit, JPA entity mapping, repository setup, and the test infrastructure needed to validate migration and entity alignment without requiring a running database.

The processed_events table is intentionally excluded from this epic — it supports Kafka idempotent consumption and belongs to the event-driven workflow epic.

Stories in this epic are sourced from:
- story-document-application-properties.json
- stories-document-schema.json (migrations, ApplicationDocument entity, DocumentRequirement and DocumentVerificationAudit entities)

The Docker Compose MySQL infrastructure story is excluded — it is tracked under a separate infrastructure epic.
userType: backend developer owning the Document Management Service
functionality: configure the service's data layer including database connectivity, schema migrations, JPA entities for document tracking, document requirements, and verification audit, and test infrastructure
benefit: so the document service has a validated persistence foundation that document upload, S3 integration, and verification workflow features can build on without schema drift between environments

stories.summary: Configure application properties across base, dev, and test profiles for document-service
stories.description:
The document-service is scaffolded with a base application.yaml in place. This story adds the required Maven dependencies for persistence, migration, and validation, defines environment variables for the document database, and populates application-dev.yaml and application-test.yaml so the service can start, connect to MySQL, run Flyway migrations, and run tests in isolation against H2 without requiring a running database instance.

Note: infra/mysql/init/01_init.sql must be updated to provision the loanflow_document_db database and grant the application user access. This depends on the Docker Compose MySQL infrastructure story.

Note: processed_events table is excluded from this epic — it is part of the Kafka idempotency pattern introduced in the event-driven workflow epic.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Service starts and runs Flyway migrations on dev profile
acceptanceCriteria.given: the MySQL container is running and environment variables are set from .env
acceptanceCriteria.when: the document-service starts with the dev profile
acceptanceCriteria.then: Flyway runs migration scripts from classpath:db/migration against loanflow_document_db and JPA validates the schema without attempting to auto-create tables

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Tests run Flyway migrations against H2 and validate entity mappings
acceptanceCriteria.given: the test profile is active and no MySQL instance is running
acceptanceCriteria.then: Flyway runs all migration scripts against H2 in-memory, Hibernate validates entity mappings against the migrated schema, and the context loads without error

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: No credentials are hardcoded in source
acceptanceCriteria.then: all connection values are resolved from environment variables with inline defaults and no plaintext credentials appear in committed files

tasks.summary: Add JPA, MySQL, Flyway, Validation, and H2 dependencies to pom.xml
tasks.description:
Add the following dependencies to the document-service pom.xml:

- spring-boot-starter-data-jpa
- spring-boot-starter-validation
- mysql-connector-j (runtime scope)
- flyway-core
- com.h2database:h2 (test scope only)

Ensure versions are compatible with Spring Boot 3.5.13.

tasks.summary: Add document database environment variables to infra/.env.example
Configure datasource, JPA, and Flyway in application-dev.yaml
tasks.description:
Populate application-dev.yaml with server port, datasource, JPA, and Flyway settings. Each connection part must use environment variable substitution with inline defaults:

server:
  port: 8083

spring:
  datasource:
    url: jdbc:mysql://${DATABASE_HOST:localhost}:${DATABASE_PORT:3306}/${DOCUMENT_DB_NAME:loanflow_document_db}
    username: ${DATABASE_USER:loanflow}
    password: ${DATABASE_PASSWORD:password}
  jpa:
    hibernate:
      ddl-auto: validate
    show-sql: true
    properties:
      hibernate:
        dialect: org.hibernate.dialect.MySQLDialect
        format_sql: true
  flyway:
    enabled: true
    locations: classpath:db/migration

tasks.summary: Configure application-test.yaml to run Flyway against H2 with schema validation
tasks.description:
Populate application-test.yaml so tests run Flyway migrations against H2 in-memory and validate entity mappings against the migrated schema without requiring MySQL or Docker:

spring:
  datasource:
    url: jdbc:h2:mem:loanflow_document_test;DB_CLOSE_DELAY=-1;MODE=MySQL;NON_KEYWORDS=VALUE
    driver-class-name: org.h2.Driver
    username: sa
    password:
  jpa:
    hibernate:
      ddl-auto: validate
    show-sql: true
  flyway:
    enabled: true
    locations: classpath:db/migration

Note: migration SQL must be written to the H2-compatible subset. Avoid ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP and ENGINE=InnoDB.

stories.summary: Create Flyway migration scripts for document service schema
stories.description: Write the SQL migration scripts that define the document service database schema across three tables: application_documents, document_requirements, and document_verification_audit. The processed_events table is excluded — it belongs to the Kafka idempotency pattern introduced in the event-driven workflow epic. Migrations run in order via Flyway against MySQL 8 in dev and against H2 in MySQL mode in the test profile. All SQL must be written to the common subset supported by both targets: avoid ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, ENGINE=InnoDB, and MySQL-specific index syntax not supported by H2. Each table is defined in its own versioned file under src/main/resources/db/migration.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Flyway runs all migrations cleanly on dev startup
acceptanceCriteria.given: the MySQL container is running and the document-service starts with the dev profile
acceptanceCriteria.then: all three migration scripts apply in order without errors and the flyway_schema_history table records each as success

acceptanceCriteria.when: the document-service starts with ddl-auto set to validate
acceptanceCriteria.then: Hibernate validates all entity mappings against the schema without throwing a validation exception

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Migrations execute against H2 in test profile and entities validate
acceptanceCriteria.given: the test profile is active with Flyway enabled against H2 and ddl-auto set to validate
acceptanceCriteria.then: Flyway runs all migration scripts against H2 without error and Hibernate validates all entity mappings against the migrated schema

tasks.description:
Define the application_documents table:

- document_id BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY
- application_id BIGINT NOT NULL
- document_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL
- file_name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL
- storage_key VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL
- content_type VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL
- file_size_bytes BIGINT NOT NULL
- upload_status VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL
- verification_status VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL
- uploaded_by_user_id BIGINT NOT NULL
- uploaded_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
- verified_at TIMESTAMP NULL

Constraints:
- UNIQUE on storage_key

Indexes:
- INDEX on application_id
- COMPOSITE INDEX on (application_id, document_type)
- INDEX on verification_status

H2 compatibility: omit ENGINE=InnoDB and DEFAULT CHARSET clauses. Do not use ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP — updated fields will be managed at the application layer.

Define the document_requirements table. This is standalone reference data that defines which document types are required per loan type:

- requirement_id BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY
- loan_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL
- document_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL
- required_flag BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE
- display_order INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0
- active_flag BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE

Constraints:
- COMPOSITE UNIQUE on (loan_type, document_type)

Seed data — insert base document requirements as part of this migration:
INSERT INTO document_requirements (loan_type, document_type, required_flag, display_order, active_flag) VALUES
  ('PERSONAL', 'GOVERNMENT_ID', TRUE, 1, TRUE),
  ('PERSONAL', 'PROOF_OF_INCOME', TRUE, 2, TRUE),
  ('PERSONAL', 'BANK_STATEMENT', TRUE, 3, TRUE),
  ('AUTO', 'GOVERNMENT_ID', TRUE, 1, TRUE),
  ('AUTO', 'PROOF_OF_INCOME', TRUE, 2, TRUE),
  ('AUTO', 'BANK_STATEMENT', TRUE, 3, TRUE),
  ('MORTGAGE', 'GOVERNMENT_ID', TRUE, 1, TRUE),
  ('MORTGAGE', 'PROOF_OF_INCOME', TRUE, 2, TRUE),
  ('MORTGAGE', 'BANK_STATEMENT', TRUE, 3, TRUE),
  ('MORTGAGE', 'TAX_RETURN', TRUE, 4, TRUE),
  ('MORTGAGE', 'PROPERTY_VALUATION', TRUE, 5, TRUE),
  ('BUSINESS', 'GOVERNMENT_ID', TRUE, 1, TRUE),
  ('BUSINESS', 'BANK_STATEMENT', TRUE, 2, TRUE),
  ('BUSINESS', 'TAX_RETURN', TRUE, 3, TRUE),
  ('BUSINESS', 'EMPLOYMENT_LETTER', TRUE, 4, TRUE);

H2 compatibility: omit ENGINE=InnoDB and DEFAULT CHARSET clauses.

Define the document_verification_audit table as an append-only record of officer verification actions:

- verification_audit_id BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY
- document_id BIGINT NOT NULL
- verified_by_user_id BIGINT NOT NULL
- verification_result VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL
- notes VARCHAR(500) NULL
- verified_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP

Constraints:
- FOREIGN KEY (document_id) REFERENCES application_documents(document_id)

Indexes:
- INDEX on document_id
- INDEX on verified_at

H2 compatibility: omit ENGINE=InnoDB and DEFAULT CHARSET clauses. Do not use ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP.

stories.summary: Create ApplicationDocument JPA entity and repository
stories.description: Implement the ApplicationDocument JPA entity and its supporting enums mapped to the application_documents table, along with its Spring Data repository. ApplicationDocument is the aggregate root for the document service — all verification audit records belong to a document. All field names, column mappings, and constraints must align with the V1 Flyway migration.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: ApplicationDocument entity validates against the migrated schema
acceptanceCriteria.given: Flyway migrations have run and ddl-auto is set to validate
acceptanceCriteria.then: Hibernate validates ApplicationDocument against application_documents without error

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Repository can persist and retrieve a document record
acceptanceCriteria.given: a valid ApplicationDocument entity is constructed
acceptanceCriteria.then: it is persisted and can be retrieved by document_id and by application_id

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Unique constraint prevents duplicate storage keys
acceptanceCriteria.given: an ApplicationDocument with a given storage_key already exists
acceptanceCriteria.when: a second ApplicationDocument is saved with the same storage_key

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Repository supports filtering by application and verification status
acceptanceCriteria.given: multiple documents exist for the same application with varying verification statuses
acceptanceCriteria.when: the repository is queried by application_id and verification_status

tasks.summary: Create DocumentType, UploadStatus, and VerificationStatus enums
tasks.description:
Create enums under com.loanflow.documentservice.entity.enums:

DocumentType: GOVERNMENT_ID, PROOF_OF_INCOME, BANK_STATEMENT, TAX_RETURN, EMPLOYMENT_LETTER, PROPERTY_VALUATION, PAY_STUB

UploadStatus: PENDING, UPLOADED, FAILED

VerificationStatus: PENDING_REVIEW, VERIFIED, REJECTED, REQUIRES_RESUBMISSION

Annotate with @Enumerated(EnumType.STRING) where used in entities to persist the string value rather than ordinal.

Create com.loanflow.documentservice.entity.ApplicationDocument mapped to the application_documents table:

- @Id @GeneratedValue documentId
- applicationId (BIGINT, not null)
- documentType (@Enumerated STRING)
- fileName (VARCHAR 255)
- storageKey (VARCHAR 500, unique)
- contentType (VARCHAR 100)
- fileSizeBytes (BIGINT)
- uploadStatus (@Enumerated STRING)
- verificationStatus (@Enumerated STRING, default PENDING_REVIEW)
- uploadedByUserId (BIGINT)
- uploadedAt (@CreationTimestamp, updatable = false)
- verifiedAt (nullable — set when verification_status transitions to VERIFIED or REJECTED)
- auditRecords: @OneToMany(mappedBy = "document", fetch = FetchType.LAZY, cascade = ALL)

Annotate with @Entity, @Table(name = "application_documents"), @Getter, @Setter, @NoArgsConstructor, @Builder from Lombok.

Create com.loanflow.documentservice.repository.ApplicationDocumentRepository extending JpaRepository<ApplicationDocument, Long>.

Include the following query methods:
- findByApplicationId(Long applicationId) returning List<ApplicationDocument>
- findByApplicationIdAndDocumentType(Long applicationId, DocumentType documentType) returning Optional<ApplicationDocument>
- findByApplicationIdAndVerificationStatus(Long applicationId, VerificationStatus status) returning List<ApplicationDocument>
- countByApplicationIdAndVerificationStatus(Long applicationId, VerificationStatus status) returning long (used to check if all required docs are verified)
- existsByStorageKey(String storageKey) returning boolean

stories.summary: Create DocumentRequirement and DocumentVerificationAudit JPA entities and repositories
stories.description: Implement the DocumentRequirement entity mapped to the document_requirements reference data table, and the DocumentVerificationAudit entity mapped to the document_verification_audit table. DocumentRequirement is standalone reference data seeded at migration time and drives the required document checklist logic. DocumentVerificationAudit is append-only and records each officer verification action against a document. All mappings must align with the V2 and V3 Flyway migrations.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: DocumentRequirement and DocumentVerificationAudit validate against migrated schema
acceptanceCriteria.given: all three Flyway migrations have run and ddl-auto is validate
acceptanceCriteria.then: Hibernate validates both entities without error

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Document requirements are queryable by loan type
acceptanceCriteria.given: seed data from V2 migration has been applied
acceptanceCriteria.when: the repository is queried for active requirements for loan type MORTGAGE
acceptanceCriteria.then: all five MORTGAGE requirement records are returned ordered by display_order

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Verification audit records are appended without modifying the document
acceptanceCriteria.given: an ApplicationDocument exists with verificationStatus PENDING_REVIEW
acceptanceCriteria.when: an officer saves a DocumentVerificationAudit record referencing that document
acceptanceCriteria.then: the audit record is persisted and retrievable by document_id without altering the original document row

tasks.description:
Create enum under com.loanflow.documentservice.entity.enums:

VerificationResult: APPROVED, REJECTED, REQUIRES_RESUBMISSION

Annotate with @Enumerated(EnumType.STRING) where used in entities.

Create com.loanflow.documentservice.entity.DocumentRequirement mapped to the document_requirements table. This is reference data and should not be modified at runtime:

- @Id @GeneratedValue requirementId
- loanType (@Enumerated STRING — reuses LoanType enum from shared context, or use a local String field)
- documentType (@Enumerated STRING)
- requiredFlag (BOOLEAN)
- displayOrder (INT)
- activeFlag (BOOLEAN)

Annotate with @Entity, @Table(name = "document_requirements"), @Getter, @NoArgsConstructor, @Builder from Lombok. Omit @Setter to discourage runtime mutation.

Create DocumentRequirementRepository extending JpaRepository<DocumentRequirement, Long>:
- findByLoanTypeAndActiveFlagTrueOrderByDisplayOrderAsc(String loanType) returning List<DocumentRequirement>
- findByLoanTypeAndDocumentTypeAndActiveFlagTrue(String loanType, DocumentType documentType) returning Optional<DocumentRequirement>

Create com.loanflow.documentservice.entity.DocumentVerificationAudit mapped to document_verification_audit. This entity is append-only — no update operations should be performed on it:

- @Id @GeneratedValue verificationAuditId
- @ManyToOne @JoinColumn(name = "document_id") document
- verifiedByUserId (BIGINT)
- verificationResult (@Enumerated STRING)
- notes (VARCHAR 500, nullable)
- verifiedAt (@CreationTimestamp, updatable = false)

Annotate with @Entity, @Table(name = "document_verification_audit"), @Getter, @NoArgsConstructor, @Builder from Lombok. Omit @Setter to enforce append-only access pattern.

Create DocumentVerificationAuditRepository extending JpaRepository<DocumentVerificationAudit, Long>:
- findByDocument_DocumentIdOrderByVerifiedAtAsc(Long documentId) returning List<DocumentVerificationAudit>
- findTopByDocument_DocumentIdOrderByVerifiedAtDesc(Long documentId) returning Optional<DocumentVerificationAudit>
